notes: This pass continued from the repo-forward seam at C3:2700 using the same caller-plus-local-structure discipline as the recent conservative C3 pages.

The page produces only one visible raw target, C3:A1B7 -> C3:2709, and that apparent support is rejected because the caller neighborhood at A1B7 reads as obvious byte-soup / inline-data material rather than trustworthy code.

A tiny-veneer scan does not rescue the page; it only surfaces unsupported BRA-style landings at 2714, 2771, 2787, 27A8, and 27BF, all of which sit inside unstable mixed byte streams rather than defining a defendable true start.
There is no clean RTS, RTL, RTI, JSR-RTS, or JSL-RTS executable splinter anywhere inside 2700..27FF.

The middle/late portion of the page looks denser and more control/arithmetic-like than some of the immediately previous pointer-heavy freezes, but that apparent coherence still breaks repeatedly on inline 00 bytes and unsupported interior branch landings.

Because the only visible raw target is caller-invalid and the more code-looking local regions still have no caller-backed true start, the whole 2700..27FF page is frozen honestly as mixed inline content before the next 2800 seam.
